26139.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This ordinal number is formed by adding the suffix "-tama" to the cardinal number "cattālīsa" (forty). The contextual sentence from SN 33.36-40 simply lists the term, likely as part of a numerical sequence.

55168.meaning_1: the set of five discourses beginning with 'rūpasamapekkhaṇa'
55168.meaning_lit: discourse on looking away from material form, etc., pentad
55168.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The compound refers to a set of five suttas (SN 33.41-45) in the Vacchagotta Saṃyutta, whose titles all begin with variations on the term 'asamapekkhaṇa' (not contemplating, not viewing) regarding rūpa, vedanā, etc. The term is a technical compound describing a specific sutta group.

9516.meaning_1: not paying proper attention; improper consideration
9516.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence from SN 33.41-45, "rūpe ... asamapekkhaṇā" is used to explain that speculative views arise *because of not examining* (form, feeling, etc.). This illustrates the noun is used as the abstract cause or reason for wrong view, corresponding to the instrumental case ending -ā.
9516.sutta_1: “rūpe kho, vaccha, asamapekkhaṇā …pe… viññāṇe kho, vaccha, asamapekkhaṇā …pe….
9516.example_1: “Vaccha, it is because of not examining form …”

39791.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
From the contextual sentence in SN 33.41-45, this ordinal number is used to designate a sequence (e.g., the forty-fifth in a series of questions or items). The form is a regular ordinal derivative from the cardinal number pañcacattālīsa (forty-five).

55166.meaning_1: The pentad of discourses on non-contemplation of form, etc.
55166.meaning_lit: discourse on non-contemplation of form, etc. in pentad
55166.compound_construction: rūpassa appaccupekkhaṇā ādi etesu suttānaṃ pañcakaṃ
55166.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a descriptive compound naming a set of five suttas (SN33.46-50). The core terms "appaccupekkhaṇā" (non-contemplation) refer to the answers Vacchagotta gives to the Buddha's questions about form, etc.

6788.meaning_1: not thinking about; not reviewing; not re-considering
6788.meaning_2: lack of scrutiny; failure to examine; non-investigation
6788.construction: na > a + pati > pacc + upa + √ikkh + ana > aṇa
6788.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence (SN 33.46-50), "appaccupekkhaṇā" (ablative singular) is given as the reason for various speculative views. The meaning is the absence or failure of the act of careful looking (paccupekkhaṇa), i.e., not scrutinizing or investigating (form, etc.), which leads to confusion.
6788.sutta_1: “rūpe kho, vaccha, appaccupekkhaṇā …pe… viññāṇe kho, vaccha, appaccupekkhaṇā …pe….
6788.example_1: “Vaccha, it is because of not scrutinizing form …”

6779.meaning_1: not making clear; not making visible; not making evident
6779.meaning_2: not directly experiencing; lacking direct knowledge of
6779.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the provided sentence, the term is used in a philosophical context (SN 33.51) to describe a state of not having direct, evident knowledge regarding the Four Noble Truths as applied to form, etc. The translation "not directly experiencing" captures the epistemic sense of paccakkha (directly evident, clear to the senses). The compound is a negative kammadhāraya (adjective-noun compound).
6779.sutta_1: “rūpe kho, vaccha, appaccakkhakammā, rūpasamudaye appaccakkhakammā, rūpanirodhe appaccakkhakammā, rūpanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya appaccakkhakammā …pe….
6779.example_1: “Vaccha, it is because of not directly experiencing form …

67695.meaning_1: the Discourse on Consciousness as Imperceptible Action
67695.meaning_lit: discourse on consciousness as imperceptible action
67695.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The compound refers to a sutta discussing the nature of consciousness (viññāṇa) as something that is not directly perceived (apaccakkha) and its relationship to action (kamma). The headword is likely the title of a sutta in the Vacchagottasaṃyutta.

59659.meaning_1: The Discourse on the Attainment Based on Concentration
59659.meaning_lit: discourse on attainment having concentration as basis
59659.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
Based on contextual sentence SN34.1, this is the first sutta in the Jhāna Saṃyutta concerning concentration (samādhi). The title indicates a teaching about attainments (samāpatti) that have concentration as their foundation (mūlaka).

59656.meaning_1: the discourse on remaining based on concentration
59656.meaning_lit: discourse on (right) concentration, foundation, and establishment
59656.compound_construction: samādhi ca mūlaṃ ca kaṭhiti ca suttaṃ ca
59656.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the second sutta in the Samādhi Saṃyutta (SN 34). The title is a list (dvanda) of the topics it addresses: concentration (samādhi), its foundation (mūla), and its establishment/right persistence (kaṭhiti).

59661.meaning_1: Discourse on the bases [for determination] grounded in concentration
59661.meaning_lit: discourse on the basis or foundation in concentration
59661.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta deals with the various bases or foundations (vuṭṭhāna) for emerging from jhāna, all rooted in concentration (samādhimūlaka). The compound is a tappurisa (determinative), with the core being "vuṭṭhāna-sutta" (discourse on emergence), modified by "samādhi-mūlaka" (having concentration as its root/foundation). The meaning is derived from SN34.3, which discusses different meditators based on their skill in concentration and the basis for emerging from it.

59654.meaning_1: discourse on being skilled in the basis of concentration
59654.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta title refers to a discourse where the Buddha explains the quality of a meditator who is skilled in the basis or foundation (mūla) of concentration (samādhi). The term "kallita" (skilled, dexterous) is a past participle of "kal" (to be able). Based on the provided contextual reference, the sutta is number 4 in the Jhāna Saṃyutta (SN 34).

59655.meaning_1: the discourse on the basis and range (of samādhi)
59655.meaning_lit: discourse on meditation as basis and pasture
59655.compound_construction: samādhimūla ca gocara ca, taṃ suttaṃ
59655.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The contextual placement (SN34.6) and the headword ending in "sutta" confirm it is a sutta title. The literal analysis is a compound of samādhi (concentration/meditation), mūla (basis/root), and gocara (pasture/range/domain). The family_set field is correct. The compound may be a kammadhāraya describing the sutta, but the internal structure of "samādhimūlakagocara" appears to be a dvanda (copulative compound) "basis and range of samādhi", qualifying the main word "sutta".

59652.meaning_1: Discourse on Application Rooted in Concentration
59652.meaning_lit: discourse on application rooted in concentration
59652.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta title is a complex compound describing a discourse (sutta) concerning application (abhinīhāra) that has concentration (samādhi) as its root (mūlaka). It is the 7th sutta in the Jhānasaṃyutta (connected discourses on meditative absorptions), part of the Saṃyutta Nikāya.

59657.meaning_1: sutta on the meditator who is thorough in the practice with concentration as foundation
59657.meaning_lit: discourse on the thorough worker in the practice with concentration as foundation
59657.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the eighth sutta of the Jhāna Saṃyutta (SN34.8). The title describes a meditator whose practice (kārī) is thorough (sakkacca) and has concentration (samādhi) as its foundation (mūla).

56387.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence (samādhismiṃ sakkaccakārī), the word functions as an adjective in the locative, describing a meditator who does not act carefully or respectfully with regard to immersion. The addition of "+loc" to the `plus_case` field reflects this syntactic usage common for the *-kārī compounds in this set. The meaning emphasizes attentive, diligent, or respectful action towards the object (samādhi).
56387.sutta_1: idha, bhikkhave, ekacco jhāyī samādhismiṃ samādhikusalo hoti, na samādhismiṃ sakkaccakārī.
56387.example_1: One meditator is skilled in immersion but not in practicing carefully for it. …”

59660.meaning_1: discourse on persistence as the basis of concentration
59660.meaning_lit: discourse on one who persists (in work) with concentration as the basis
59660.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This sutta title is a multi-element descriptive compound. The core is `sātaccakārī` (a doer of persistence), described as having `samādhimūlaka` (concentration as its basis). It is the 9th sutta of the Jhāna Saṃyutta (SN34.9). The literal parsing suggests a subject who works/acts (kārī) persistently (sātacca) with their root/basis (mūlaka) being concentration (samādhi).

59658.meaning_1: The discourse on the one acting fitly regarding the foundation of concentration; the discourse on the one acting fitly rooted in concentration
59658.meaning_lit: discourse on the one acting fitly regarding the foundation of concentration
59658.family_compound: samādhi mūla sappa kasappāya kārī sutta
59658.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
A compound sutta title from the Jhānasaṃyutta, specifically dealing with the root or foundation (mūlaka) of concentration (samādhi) and the one (kārī) acting fitly or suitably (sappāya). The contextual reference SN34.10 confirms its position and the standard naming pattern for suttas (ending in -sutta). The construction is a series of qualifiers (samādhimūlakasappāyakārī) for the neuter noun sutta.

59744.meaning_1: Discourse on Standing at the Root of Attainment
59744.meaning_lit: discourse on standing at the root of attainment
59744.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta name is a compound meaning "the discourse on standing at the root of attainment." It is Sutta 11 of the Jhāna Saṃyutta (SN 34.11). The compound is analyzed as a genitive tappurisa (chaṭṭhī tappurisa) with *samāpattimūla* (the root/base of attainment) being the base upon which *ṭhiti* (standing/remaining) occurs, and *sutta* is the final member.

59748.meaning_1: Discourse on Emergence Based on Attainment
59748.meaning_lit: discourse on the basis of attainment and emerging
59748.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This sutta title from the Jhānasaṃyutta (SN 34) is a dvanda compound. It refers to the twin themes of the discourse: the basis (or cause) for attaining jhāna (samāpattimūlaka) and the emerging from it (vuṭṭhāna). The contextual citation confirms its placement.

59742.meaning_1: discourse on one who is proficient in the basis of attainment
59742.meaning_lit: discourse on one who is proficient in the basis of attainment
59742.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the Saṃyutta Nikāya context (Jhānasaṃyutta), this sutta discusses a meditator who is skilled (kallita) with respect to the basis or foundation (mūla) of meditative attainment (samāpatti). The meaning is derived from the compound's components within the subject matter of the chapter.

59741.meaning_lit: discourse on object as source of attainment
59741.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the name of the 14th sutta in the Jhāna Saṃyutta (SN 34.14). The title refers to a discourse analyzing meditative attainment (samāpatti) based on its source/root (mūlaka) or object (ārammaṇa).

59740.meaning_1: The discourse on the Foundation in Attainment Concerning Application of Will
59740.meaning_lit: discourse on the foundation in attainment concerning application of will
59740.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The compound is a descriptive compound (kammadhāraya) where "samāpattimūlakaabhinīhāra" describes the type of "sutta". "samāpattimūlaka" (founded on/rooted in attainment) qualifies "abhinīhāra" (application of will, resolution). The title thus refers to a sutta discussing the application of will based on meditative attainment, fitting the context of SN 34.16 within the Jhāna Saṃyutta.

59745.meaning_1: The Discourse on the Root of Attainment and Care
59745.meaning_lit: discourse on the root of attainment and care
59745.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This sutta title is a tatpurusa compound analyzing the qualities related to meditative attainment (samāpatti). It is Sutta 17 of the Samāpatti Saṃyutta (SN 34.17). The context indicates it discusses a meditator who is skilled in the foundation of attainment (samāpattimūla) and in careful practice (sakkacca).

59747.meaning_1: discourse on persistence based on the foundation of attainment
59747.meaning_lit: discourse on the foundation of attainment and persistence
59747.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the name of the eighteenth sutta in the Jhānasaṃyutta (chapter on jhāna) within the Saṃyutta Nikāya (SN 34.18). It deals with the qualities of a meditator regarding the foundation of attainment (samāpatti) and persistence (sātacca). Based on the structure of other sutta titles in the same collection (e.g., samāpattikusala, samāpattimūlaka), the compound is likely a dvanda (co-ordinative) meaning a discourse concerning both the foundation of attainment and persistence.

59746.meaning_1: Discourse on One Who Does What is Conducive Rooted in Attainment
59746.meaning_lit: discourse on one who does what is conducive rooted in attainment
59746.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a sutta title. The compound describes a discourse (sutta) about the person who is a doer (kārī) of what is suitable/conducive (sappāya), which is rooted in (mūlaka) attainment (samāpatti). In the context of SN34.19, it belongs to the Jhāna Saṃyutta.

29124.meaning_1: The group of eight suttas beginning with the 'Discourse on Staying as a Basis and ...' (etc.); The eight including the suttas on staying, basis, and origin.
29124.meaning_lit: discourse on staying as a basis and ... (etc.), and eight others
29124.family_compound: ṭhiti mūla kā vuṭṭhāna sutta ādi aṭṭha
29124.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a collective title for a set of eight suttas within the Jhānasaṃyutta (SN 34). The contextual sentence indicates it refers to the sequence from SN 34.20 to SN 34.27. The compound breaks down into 'ṭhitimūlakavuṭṭhānasutta' (the first sutta's title), 'ādi' (beginning with), and 'aṭṭhaka' (a group of eight), thus meaning 'the eight [suttas] beginning with the Ṭhitimūlakavuṭṭhānasutta'.

69998.meaning_1: a set of seven suttas, starting with the Vuṭṭhāna, Mūlaka, and Kallita suttas
69998.meaning_lit: discourse on the set of seven, beginning with the discourses on emergence, root, and settled
69998.family_compound: vuṭṭhāna mūlaka kallita sutta ādi sattaka
69998.construction: vuṭṭhāna + mūlaka + kallita + sutta + ādi + sattaka
69998.compound_construction: vuṭṭhānasutta ca mūlakasutta ca kallitasutta ca ādi ca sattakaṃ
69998.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a compound title for a group of seven discourses (suttas) within the Jhānasaṃyutta of the Saṃyutta Nikāya. It is a dvanda compound enumerating the starting suttas in the set: Vuṭṭhāna, Mūlaka, and Kallita. The contextual citation shows it in a listing format.

1822.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence (SN 34.28-34), the word appears in the neuter accusative singular, qualifying a series of meditation attainments or factors, indicating the "twenty-eighth" item in a sequence. The form "aṭṭhavīsatimaṃ" confirms the stem "aṭṭhavīsatim-" for the ordinal.

20718.meaning_1: the six including the suttas beginning with the base of kalliṭa
20718.meaning_lit: the group-of-six on the sutta(s) beginning with "kalliṭa" as root/object
20718.family_compound: kalliṭa mūla ārammaṇa sutta ādi cha
20718.construction: kalliṭa + mūla + ārammaṇa + sutta + ādi + cha + ka
20718.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a title for a group of six suttas (SN 34.35-40) within the Jhāna Saṃyutta. The compound's core is "kalliṭamūlakaārammaṇasuttādi", referring to suttas starting with the one about "kalliṭa" (threshed, hammered, prepared) as a "mūlaka" (base/foundation) or "ārammaṇa" (object of meditation). The "cha" (six) + "ka" (taddhita suffix for grouping) forms a collective dvigu compound, meaning "the group-of-six consisting of...".

12427.meaning_1: Group of Five Discourses including the Discourses on Basis of Meditation Object and Pasture
12427.meaning_lit: discourse on the five including the discourse on basis, root, and pasture
12427.family_compound: ārammaṇa mūla gocara sutta ādi pañcaka
12427.construction: ārammaṇa + mūla + gocara + sutta + ādi + pañcaka
12427.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
A compound title for a group of five texts in the Jhānasaṃyutta. The meaning is derived from the contextual citation SN 34.41-45. The compound is a dvanda, listing the types of discourses (sutta) included in the group. ādi indicates that the list is not exhaustive and more suttas of a similar type are included in the set of five.

17421.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the ordinal form of the cardinal number ekacattālīsa ("forty-one"). In the contextual sentence "ekacattālīsamaṃ", it is in the accusative singular neuter, likely meaning "the forty-first (item)".

25249.meaning_1: group of four discourses: the discourse on the range, the discourse on the root, and the discourse on the undertaking, etc.
25249.meaning_lit: discourse on range, root, and undertaking, etc. (in a) group of four
25249.family_compound: gocara mūlaka abhinīhāra sutta ādi catukka
25249.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a proper noun for a group of four suttas (SN 34.46-49). It is a dvanda compound listing key topics (gocara, mūlaka, abhinīhāra) of the suttas, with 'ādi' (etc.) and 'catukka' (group of four). In the contextual sentence, it refers to the specific suttas grouped together.

57700.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The word is an ordinal number formed by adding the suffix -ma to the cardinal number sattacattālīsa (forty-seven). In the contextual sentence (sn34.47:1.3), it denotes the forty-seventh item in a sequence, likely the forty-seventh samādhi practice.

1757.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The contextual sentence "aṭṭhacattālīsamaṃ." uses the neuter singular accusative form, meaning "the forty-eighth (item)". As an ordinal number, it follows the standard pattern of adding "-ma" to the cardinal number aṭṭhacattālīsa.

17788.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence "ekūnapaññāsamaṃ," the word is in the accusative singular neuter, agreeing with an implied noun (e.g., vassaṃ). It functions as the ordinal number "forty-ninth." The derivation is from the cardinal "ekūnapaññāsa" (forty-nine) plus the ordinal suffix "-ma."

17568.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The contextual sentence shows its use in a sequential list (SN 34.51). The lemma is a taddhita derivative meaning "the fifty-first one". It is correctly formed from the cardinal number `ekapaññāsa` (fifty-one) by adding the ordinal suffix `-ma`.

56389.meaning_1: the duplet (or couple) of suttas (starting with) the ones on [the jhāna factor of] application of mind with care (sakkacca), etc., i.e., with root (mūlaka), with persistence (sātaccakārī)
56389.meaning_lit: discourse on [the jhāna factor of] application of mind with care, etc., and on the root, and on the doer of persistence
56389.family_compound: sakkacca mūlaka sātaccakārī sutta ādi duka
56389.construction: sakkacca + mūlaka + sātaccakārī + sutta + ādi + duka
56389.compound_construction: sakkaccasuttañ ca mūlakasuttañ ca sātaccakārīsuttañ ca ādisuttādiñ ca dukañ ca
56389.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a compound naming a specific section of the Jhānasaṃyutta (SN34.53-54). It is a dvanda (copulative) compound, listing the topics of the suttas in this duplet (duka): sakkacca (careful application), mūlaka (rootedness), and sātaccakārī (persistent practice). The form is neuter because it refers to the pair/group of suttas.

62143.meaning_1: The Connected Discourses on Concentration - The Discourse on One Who Engages in Persistence as the Foundation and What is Beneficial
62143.meaning_lit: discourse on one who engages in persistence as the foundation and what is beneficial
62143.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The lemma is a sutta name. The contextual source is Saṃyutta Nikāya, Jhāna Saṃyutta, sutta 55. The compound contains "sātaccamūlaka" (having persistence as foundation), "sappāyakārī" (one who does what is beneficial), and "sutta". The provided meaning_1 is inferred from the title of the sutta collection and the meaning of the component words.

23442.meaning_1: (in Buddhist doctrine) the five aggregates of clinging; the psychophysical components of existence
23442.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence from SN 34.55, khandha refers to the doctrinal "aggregates," likely forming part of the compound "Khandhasaṃyutta," a chapter of the Saṃyutta Nikāya. This entry is a specific doctrinal sense distinct from the earlier numbered entries for khandha.

66411.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence from Sn34.55, "valāha" is found in a compound "vacchajhāna". While the exact parsing of this compound is not standard, the headword "valāha" maintains its core meaning of "cloud", here likely referring to a type of meditation or jhāna associated with clouds, perhaps indicating a meditative state of calm and shade.

23470.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The contextual sentence confirms this is a title, specifically the closing line of the Khandhavagga section of the Saṃyutta Nikāya. The compound is a *kammadhāraya* where the prior members *khandhavagga* and *saṃyutta* modify the final head noun *pāḷi* (text).
23470.example_1: The Book of the Aggregates is finished.

61519.meaning_1: Book 4 of the Saṃyutta Nikāya; Book of the Six Sense Fields
61519.meaning_2: The chapter on the six sense bases (the first fifty suttas).
61519.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence, "saḷāyatanavagge paṭhamapaṇṇāsako samatto" means "The first fifty (suttas) in the Saḷāyatanavagga is completed." This confirms it is a chapter title. Lemma_1 was corrected to remove the extraneous " 2".

1295.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the name of a sutta (discourse). The headword is a compound consisting of ajjhatta (internal) + anicca (impermanence) + sutta (discourse). The meaning is "The Sutta on Internal Impermanence" or "The Impermanence (Discourse) regarding the Internal (Sense Bases)". The contextual reference SN35.1 confirms this is the first discourse in the Saṃyutta Nikāya, Salayatanasaṃyutta, discussing the impermanence of the internal sense bases.

1266.meaning_1: Discourse on the Internal as Suffering
1266.meaning_lit: discourse on the internal as suffering
1266.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the second sutta in the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (The Connected Discourses on the Six Sense Bases). The title derives from its opening phrase, "Ajjhattaṃ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṃ," and it explains the six internal sense bases (eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, mind) as impermanent and therefore suffering.

1286.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The compound "ajjhattānatta" is a kammadhāraya compound meaning "internal not-self". It serves as a description for the "sutta" (discourse), hence forming a descriptive compound for the sutta title. The meaning of the sutta title can be understood as "The Discourse on the Internal Not-Self", referring to the teaching that the six internal sense bases (eye, ear, nose, tongue, body, mind) are not-self (anattā). Based on SN35.3, the meaning_1 field was left empty as no direct English translation was provided in the contextual sentence. The literal meaning is constructed accordingly.

48367.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a sutta title. The word is a compound describing a discourse about the impermanence (anicca) of external (bāhira) phenomena, as found in the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (the Connected Discourses on the Six Sense Bases). The lemma_2 is given as the nominative singular, but as a neuter noun it should match lemma_1.

48347.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The headword is a compound noun denoting a specific discourse (sutta) from the Saṃyutta Nikāya. Based on the contextual reference "SN35.5" and the provided "family_set", this is a title within the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta. The literal meaning is constructed from "bāhira" (external), "dukkha" (suffering), and "sutta" (discourse).

48362.meaning_1: sutta on the external as not-self
48362.meaning_lit: discourse on the external as not-self
48362.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the name of the sixth sutta in the Saḷāyatana Saṃyutta. The title refers to the teaching that the external sense bases (bāhirāni āyatanāni) are not-self (anatta).

1297.meaning_1: The Discourse on the Internally Impermanent in the Past and Future
1297.meaning_lit: discourse on the internal, impermanent, past, and future
1297.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This sutta title is a compound describing its subject matter: a teaching (sutta) concerning the internal (ajjhatta) sense bases, focusing on their impermanent (anicca) nature as it applies to the past (atīta) and the future (anāgata). It belongs to the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta, which deals with the six sense bases.

1268.meaning_1: discourse on internal misery past and future
1268.meaning_lit: discourse on internal misery past and future
1268.compound_construction: ajjhatta + dukkhaṃ ca atītaṃ ca anāgataṃ ca
1268.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta is from the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (SN 35.8) and deals with the six internal sense bases, characterizing them as dukkha (unsatisfactory) whether in the past, future, or present.

1288.meaning_1: The Discourse on the Internal as Not-Self in the Past and Future
1288.meaning_lit: discourse on the internal not-self past and future
1288.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The headword is a title of a sutta. It is a dvanda (copulative) compound of "ajjhattānattā" (internal not-self) and "atītānāgata" (past and future), together modifying "sutta". The contextual reference SN35.9 confirms this as a sutta within the Saḷāyatana-saṃyutta, discussing the six internal sense bases as not-self in past and future time frames.

48369.meaning_1: The External, Impermanent, Past and Future Discourse
48369.meaning_lit: discourse on the external, the impermanent, the past, and the future
48369.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta title is a compound describing its subject matter: a teaching about external sense bases that are impermanent, past, and future.

48349.meaning_1: The External Suffering Past and Future Discourse; The Sutta on External Suffering, Past and Future
48349.meaning_lit: discourse on external suffering, past and future
48349.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
A sutta from the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (SN 35.11) dealing with suffering in relation to the six external sense bases, considered in terms of past and future time.

48364.meaning_lit: discourse on the external as not-self, past and future
48364.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a compound sutta title from the Saṃyutta Nikāya. The term 'bāhira' (external) refers to the six external sense bases (forms, sounds, etc.). The sutta likely discusses the nature of these external bases as not-self (anatta) in the past (atīta) and future (anāgata) as per SN 35.12.

53779.meaning_1: the Yamaka (Twin) Vagga; the chapter of (or dealing with) twins/pairs
53779.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the provided context sentence, "yamakavaggo dutiyo", this entry refers to the second chapter of a larger work, specifically one of the "vaggas" of the Saṃyutta Nikāya. The compound is analyzed as a tappurisa (determinative), where the prior member "yamaka" (twin) qualifies the type of "vagga" (chapter). The meaning is a chapter consisting of or dealing with pairs/twins.

41315.meaning_1: The First Discourse on Before the Awakening
41315.meaning_lit: discourse on the first, before the enlightenment
41315.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the name of a sutta (SN 35.13). The compound can be analyzed as a descriptive compound (kammadhāraya) where "paṭhama" (first) qualifies the entire following phrase "pubbesambodhasutta" (discourse on before the enlightenment). The context shows it is the first in a series of discourses on the theme of "before the awakening" within the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta.

33326.meaning_1: The Discourse on the Second Before Enlightenment
33326.meaning_2: The second discourse (the second one) on the time before enlightenment
33326.meaning_lit: discourse on the second [topic] before enlightenment
33326.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the second of two discourses (dutiya) set in the time before the Buddha’s enlightenment (pubbe-sambodha). The compound is a kammadhāraya (descriptive compound) describing the sutta.

41172.meaning_1: The First Discourse on the Search for Gratification
41172.meaning_lit: discourse on the first search for gratification
41172.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The contextual text (SN35.15) identifies this as the third sutta in its section. The compound is a descriptive (kammadhāraya) title meaning "The First Search for Gratification Discourse". The literal components are: paṭhama (first), assāda (gratification, enjoyment), pariyesanā (search, quest), sutta (discourse).

33146.meaning_1: discourse on (the) second (topic of) seeking gratification
33146.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This sutta is the second discourse in the series on "seeking gratification" (assādapariyesana), following the first one. The contextual reference SN35.16 confirms its placement in the Saḷāyatana Saṃyutta. The lemma_2 is identical to lemma_1 as it is a neuter noun (sutta) title.

41285.meaning_1: the first discourse on gratification regarding the eye; the First Discourse on the Satisfaction in the Eye
41285.meaning_lit: discourse on the first gratification of the eye
41285.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the title of SN 35.17. The compound paṭhamanoceassāda splits as paṭhama + no + ceassāda (cakkhu-assāda). The contextual entry shows it is the 5th sutta in the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta. The literal meaning is the first (discourse) concerning the gratification of the eye.

33292.meaning_1: The Second Discourse on the Pleasure (or the Second Discourse on the Non-Delight in Pleasure)
33292.meaning_lit: discourse on the second [point about] pleasure, or non-delight
33292.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
Analysis of the compound: "dutiya" (second), "no" (not), "ce" (if, whether), "assāda" (pleasure, enjoyment), "sutta" (discourse). The title refers to the second sutta in the "Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta" section (SN 35.18) that discusses the theme of "no ce assāda" – likely analyzing the absence of pleasure or delight in the senses. The grammatical parsing is complex due to the inclusion of indeclinables "no" and "ce".

41493.meaning_lit: discourse on (delight in) the first (six sense bases)
41493.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The headword is a neuter compound noun, a sutta title. The contextual sentence 'SN35.19 saḷāyatanasaṃyuttaṃ, paṭhamābhinandasuttaṃ 7. paṭhamābhinandasuttaṃ' confirms its classification as a discourse. 'Family_set' is given, so 'meaning_1' is derived directly from it. The literal meaning interprets the components: 'paṭhama' (first), 'abhinanda' (delight/rejoicing), and 'sutta' (discourse), referring to the first discourse on delight (in the six sense bases).

33548.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
Sutta name, appears in the index for the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta. The term "abhinanda" refers to delighting in or taking pleasure in (the six sense bases).

41259.meaning_1: First Discourse on the Arising of Suffering
41259.meaning_lit: discourse on the first arising of suffering
41259.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the first (paṭhama) sutta in the Saṃyutta Nikāya sequence (SN35.21) dealing with the arising (uppāda) of suffering (dukkha). The context shows it is part of the 'saḷāyatanasaṃyuttaṃ' (the Connected Discourses on the Six Sense Bases), indicating the discourse relates to suffering arising in relation to the six senses.

33264.meaning_1: The Second (Discourse) on the Origin of Suffering
33264.meaning_lit: discourse on the second (and) arising of suffering
33264.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The headword is the title of a Buddhist scripture (Sutta). The contextual sentence shows its placement within the Saṃyutta Nikāya, specifically in the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (Connected Discourses on the Six Sense Bases). It is the second discourse (dutiya) on the arising (uppāda) of suffering (dukkha) in that collection, hence the title. The compound type is likely a Kammadhāraya (descriptive determinative) where "dutiya" qualifies "dukkhuppādasutta" (the second discourse on the arising of suffering).

58986.meaning_1: The All chapter; the chapter (vagga) on All (sabba)
58986.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
Based on the contextual sentence from SN 35.32, this is a title for a specific chapter (vagga) in the Saṃyutta Nikāya. The meaning refers to the chapter dealing with the teaching on "All" (the six senses and their objects).

58977.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is the name of the first sutta in the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (SN 35.23). It deals with the definition of "all" (sabba) as the six sense bases and their objects.

54086.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence (sotañceva saddā ca...evamitarayugalesupi), the term appears in an embedded gloss, indicating "in the other pairs likewise," i.e., the other sense bases and their objects, confirming its meaning as a "pair."
54086.example_1: kiñca, bhikkhave, sabbaṃ? cakkhuñceva rūpā ca, sotañca [sotañceva (?) evamitarayugalesupi] saddā ca, ghānañca gandhā ca, jivhā ca rasā ca, kāyo ca phoṭṭhabbā ca, mano ca dhammā ca, idaṃ vuccati, bhikkhave, sabbaṃ. yo, bhikkhave, evaṃ vadeyya, ahametaṃ sabbaṃ paccakkhāya aññaṃ sabbaṃ paññāpessāmīti, tassa vācāvatthukamevassa [vācāvatthurevassa, vācāvatthudevassa], puṭṭho ca na sampāyeyya, uttariñca vighātaṃ āpajjeyya.

45087.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a discourse in the Saṃyutta Nikāya dealing with the abandonment of the six sense bases. As the headword is marked as "pahānasutta 1", it is likely the first of two or more suttas with this title.

7688.meaning_1: Discourse on Complete Understanding and Giving Up
7688.meaning_lit: discourse on abhiññā and pariññā and pahāna
7688.compound_construction: abhiññā ca pariññā ca pahāna ca taṃ suttaṃ
7688.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The compound meaning is clear from the components: abhiññā (direct knowledge/higher knowledge), pariññā (full understanding), and pahāna (abandoning/relinquishing). The sutta (SN 35.25) likely deals with the process of fully understanding the six sense bases (saḷāyatana) through higher knowledge and then abandoning the craving and suffering associated with them.

41164.meaning_1: First Discourse on Non-Complete Understanding; First Sutta on Not Fully Knowing
41164.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
From the contextual reference SN35.26, this is a sutta title. "Aparijānana" means "non-understanding" or "not fully knowing". The sutta deals with not fully understanding the six sense bases.

33134.meaning_1: The Second Discourse on Non-Complete Understanding; Second Discourse on Not Fully Understanding
33134.meaning_lit: discourse on the second (subject of) not fully understanding
33134.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta is from the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (SN 35.27). It appears to be the second sutta on the topic of not fully understanding (aparijānana) the sense bases, following a prior sutta (pathama-) on the same topic.

3004.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
From contextual sentence SN35.29: saḷāyatanasaṃyuttaṃ, addhabhūtasuttaṃ. This sutta is part of the Saḷāyatana-saṃyutta (Connected Discourses on the Six Sense Bases). The title appears to be a bahubbīhi compound describing the discourse, possibly meaning "the discourse concerning one who is oppressed (by the six sense bases)." The word addhabhūta is a past participle from addha + √bhū, meaning "become oppressed, afflicted." The sutta title is thus "The Discourse on (the one who is) Oppressed."

3003.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence (SN 35.29), "sabbaṃ addhabhūtaṃ" is used to describe all phenomena as "overcome" or "subject to," indicating a state of being conditioned and dependent, supporting the translation "overcome."

59921.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The sutta discusses the sense bases being suitable for uprooting. The compound is a genitive tappurisa: a sutta concerning what is suitable (sāruppa) for uprooting (samugghāta).

58919.meaning_1: suitable for eradicating all conception; fit for exterminating all imagination
58919.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the given context from SN 35.30, the word describes a "practice" (paṭipadā) that is well-suited or the proper counterpart to the task of completely uprooting "all conceiving" (sabbamaññita). It follows the same pattern as related entries like paṭisallānasāruppa (suitable for seclusion) and brahmacariyasāruppa (proper for the holy life), where sāruppa (adj.) means "suitable, appropriate, conformable."
58919.sutta_1: katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasāruppā paṭipadā?
58919.example_1: And what is the practice fit for uprooting all conceiving?

41393.meaning_1: the First Discourse on Conducive to Uprooting
41393.meaning_lit: discourse on the first and that which is conducive to uprooting
41393.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a title of a sutta. It is a compound of four elements: paṭhama (first), samugghāta (uprooting, eradication), sappāya (suitable, beneficial, conducive), and sutta (discourse). The meaning in context (SN 35.31) refers to the first discourse explaining the sense bases and the things that are suitable/beneficial for the uprooting of clinging.

58918.meaning_1: beneficial for eradicating all conception; supportive for exterminating all imagination
58918.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence from SN 35.31, this adjective describes the practice (paṭipadā) that is conducive to uprooting all conceiving, highlighting its importance in the path to liberation from mental projections.
58918.sutta_1: katamā ca sā, bhikkhave, sabbamaññitasamugghātasappāyā paṭipadā?
58918.example_1: And what is the practice that’s conducive to uprooting all conceiving?

23451.meaning_2: aggregates, elements, and sense bases; aggregates, elements, and sense fields
23451.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The word is a dvanda (copulative) compound that collectively refers to the three primary analytical frameworks for classifying phenomena in early Buddhist thought. In the contextual sentence, it marks the entire domain of conditioned experience, which a practitioner should not conceive in terms of 'that', 'in that', 'as that', or 'mine'.
23451.sutta_1: yāvatā, bhikkhave, khandhadhātuāyatanaṃ tampi na maññati, tasmimpi na maññati, tatopi na maññati, taṃ meti na maññati.
23451.example_1: As far as the aggregates, elements, and sense fields extend, they don’t conceive anything to be that, they don’t conceive it in that, they don’t conceive it as that, and they don’t conceive that ‘that is mine.’

33413.meaning_1: Second Discourse on the Upheaval of the Foundations
33413.meaning_lit: discourse on the suitable for upheaval for the second
33413.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This sutta is found in the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta (SN 35.32) and deals with the removal or upheaval of the sense bases. The compound sappāya here indicates suitability or conduciveness to this upheaval, hence "Discourse on the Suitable for Upheaval for the Second."

28242.meaning_1: the chapter on being subject to birth; the section on the nature of birth
28242.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
From the contextual sentence, this is the name of the fourth vagga (chapter) of a Nikāya. It is a tatpuruṣa compound where dhamma qualifies jāti ("nature of birth"), and this compound in turn qualifies vagga ("section").

61560.meaning_1: subject to defilement; liable to corruption
61560.meaning_2: subject to defilement; liable to corruption
61560.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence SN35.33-42, "sabbaṃ, bhikkhave, saṃkilesikadhammaṃ … ." means "all, bhikkhus, is subject to defilement ...". This usage underscores the fundamental Buddhist teaching that all conditioned phenomena (sabbaṁ) are inherently susceptible to mental defilement and decay.

4158.meaning_1: the nine discourses starting with the "On the Impermanent" sutta; the group of nine suttas on impermanence, etc.
4158.meaning_lit: discourse on the nine beginning with impermanence, etc.
4158.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This refers to a specific group of nine suttas (SN 35.43-51) in the Salayatana Samyutta, which all begin with the word "anicca" (impermanent). The compound is a descriptive (kammadhāraya) meaning "the ninefold [section] of discourses having impermanence, etc., as their beginning."

7689.meaning_1: should be completely comprehended through experiential understanding; to be fully understood by direct realization
7689.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This word is a gerundive (adjective of necessity) compound. In the contextual sentence (SN 35.50), it is used predicatively with "sabbaṃ" ("all"), stating that all phenomena are "to be directly known and completely understood" (abhiññāpariññeyyaṃ). The term combines abhiññā (direct, experiential knowledge) with pariññeyya (that which should be fully comprehended), indicating a process of understanding that begins with direct insight (abhiññā) and culminates in complete penetration (pariññā).
7689.example_1: “Mendicants, all is to be directly known and completely understood. …”

16202.meaning_1: SN35.52 The discourse on (one) overcome (by passion)
16202.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a sutta title, specifically the 52nd sutta in the Saḷāyatanasaṃyutta of the Saṃyutta Nikāya. The compound consists of the past participle "upassaṭṭha" (pp of upassahati or upasadahati, meaning "overcome, afflicted, distressed") and "sutta" (discourse). The meaning in context is the title of the discourse addressing one whose senses are overcome by passion.

16200.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the provided contextual sentence from SN 35.52, the term describes "all" (sabbaṃ) phenomena as being fundamentally "contaminated" or "tainted," a key doctrinal concept often contrasted with purity.

16101.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence SN35.52, upasaṭṭha (variant reading upassaṭṭha) describes "all" (sabbaṃ) as being "afflicted". The meaning derived here is a figurative extension of the root sense "to cling to, to be attached to, to be released/let loose near," referring to the six sense bases being under the attack or influence of craving, aversion, and delusion.
16101.example_1: 52. sabbaṃ, bhikkhave, upassaṭṭhaṃ [upasaṭṭhaṃ] . kiñca, bhikkhave, sabbaṃ upassaṭṭhaṃ?

41291.meaning_2: first half-hundred; first group of fifty; the first fifty (suttas)
41291.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence (saḷāyatanavagge paṭhamapaṇṇāsako samatto), this term denotes the first major division (the first fifty suttas) of the Saḷāyatana Vagga within the Samyutta Nikāya. It functions as a masculine noun describing a collection of discourses.

40088.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The contextual snippet "paññāsamaṃ" is in the accusative singular, agreeing with a neuter noun like vāraṃ (time/occasion) or kappaṃ (aeon), meaning "the fiftieth (time/occasion)". This is the standard ordinal formation from the cardinal number paññāsa (fifty).

55164.meaning_1: the quartet of discourses starting with the discourse on the visible nature of form, etc.
55164.family_compound: rūpa appaccakkha kamma ādi sutta catukka
55164.construction: rūpa + appaccakkha + kamma + ādi + sutta + catukka
55164.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The lemma is a compound title of a set of four suttas (catukka) within the Vacchagottasaṃyutta. Based on the contextual citation (SN33.51-55), it refers to suttas discussing topics related to "rūpa" (form) being "appaccakkha" (not directly experiencable) and "kamma" (action), etc. The headword is the name of the group itself, hence its meaning describes the content of the sutta quartet. The form is neuter because "catukka" (quartet) is neuter.

59653.meaning_1: sutta on the foundation and object of concentration
59653.meaning_lit: discourse on the foundation and object of concentration
59653.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This is a compound sutta title from the Jhāna Saṃyutta (SN 34). The contextual location is SN34.5, specifying its place within the section. The compound consists of three substantive elements modifying 'sutta'. Based on standard usage in sutta titles, 'mūlaka' (foundational) and 'ārammaṇa' (object, support) are terms describing aspects of 'samādhi' (concentration). The title thus refers to a discourse concerning concentration's foundation and its object.

59743.meaning_1: discourse on the attainment and the range of the mind
59743.meaning_lit: discourse on the attainment, the basis, and the range
59743.compound_construction: samāpatti ca mūla ca gocara ca suttaṃ
59743.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
This sutta is part of the Jhānasaṃyutta (Connected Discourses on Jhāna) in the Saṃyutta Nikāya. The compound refers to a discourse dealing with meditation attainments (samāpatti), their basis (mūla), and the range or pasture (gocara) of the mind. The contextual listing indicates it is the 15th sutta in that saṃyutta.

13483.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
In the contextual sentence from SN35.13, it is used in the commentary portion (in brackets) as a grammatical example term. It refers to the indeclinable particle `iti` itself. The compound is a kammadhāraya (determinative) where `iti` is defined by `sadda` (word). Consistent with related terms `athasadda`, `abhisadda`, `khosadda`.
13483.example_1: yāvakīvañcāhaṃ, bhikkhave, imesaṃ channaṃ ajjhattikānaṃ āyatanānaṃ evaṃ assādañca assādato, ādīnavañca ādīnavato, nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṃ nābbhaññāsiṃ, neva tāvāhaṃ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya anuttaraṃ sammāsambodhiṃ abhisambuddhoti [sabbatthāpi evameva itisaddena saha dissati] paccaññāsiṃ. yato ca khvāhaṃ, bhikkhave, imesaṃ channaṃ ajjhattikānaṃ āyatanānaṃ evaṃ assādañca assādato, ādīnavañca ādīnavato, nissaraṇañca nissaraṇato yathābhūtaṃ abbhaññāsiṃ, athāhaṃ, bhikkhave, sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya anuttaraṃ sammāsambodhiṃ abhisambuddhoti paccaññāsiṃ.

28243.meaning_1: a group of ten discourses starting with "subject to birth"
28243.meaning_lit: discourse on birth, etc., set of ten
28243.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
A compound noun used as the title for a set of ten suttas (SN 35.33-42). The first word in the set is "jātidhammā" (subject to birth). The suffix "-ka" forms an adjective meaning "consisting of" or "related to," which here nominalizes the group.

58775.meaning_1: the chapter on all being impermanent
58775.comment:
openrouter: deepseek/deepseek-v3.2
The contextual sentence (sn35.52:1.17) identifies this as the fifth vagga (chapter) in a sequence. This is a descriptive compound naming a chapter in the Saṃyutta Nikāya, with the theme "all (things) are impermanent".
